title: (the-environment) — compile-time form that materializes the lexical scope
goal: Implement MIT Scheme's (the-environment) form. Returns a first-class environment value containing every lexically-visible binding (locals + globals via parent pointer). Compiles to a call to a new runtime helper eshkol_make_local_env(name1, val1, ..., parent_env) where the parser enumerates the in-scope names. (eval expr (the-environment)) then evaluates expr in that lexical scope. Decision locked in .swarm/decisions.md: compile-time form, not a runtime procedure; locals walk first then fall through to interaction-environment.
next_actions: Parser: recognize (the-environment) as a special form (zero arguments). Emit a new ESHKOL_THE_ENVIRONMENT_OP.
Codegen: walk the lexical scope chain at the form's position; emit code that calls eshkol_make_local_env with every visible (symbol, value) pair plus the interaction-environment as the parent.
Runtime: eshkol_make_local_env(n_pairs, [name, value]*, parent_env) builds a tagged environment value. eshkol_eval_env already exists; extend it to walk local pairs first, fall through to parent.

Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/the_environment_test.esk covering: simple let-scope capture, capture inside lambda, capture across nested let, eval-in-captured-env sees locals + globals, captured env survives the let's lexical lifetime.

Update docs/breakdown/SCHEME_COMPATIBILITY.md: mention (the-environment) as an MIT extension Eshkol supports (R7RS doesn't require it; SICP doesn't use it; but it's the natural primitive for embedded scripting).
acceptance: Snapshot semantics: (eval '(set! x 5) <captured-env>) does NOT mutate the surrounding lexical x.
ux_payoff: Unlocks the embedded-scripting / REPL-in-context / DSL-hosting pattern — currently impossible to write idiomatically in Eshkol.
